method_atom: a precision phase-sensitive interferometric sensor, mechanically decoupled from a driven rotating source, that detects a small anomalous coupling appearing only once a control parameter is driven past a critical threshold; the anomalous response scales linearly with the applied drive rate, grows in magnitude as the control parameter moves further beyond threshold, and exhibits a directional (parity-violating) asymmetry that depends on the sign of the drive relative to a fixed background reference, with candidate systematic confounds excluded by control experiments.
object_atom: fiber optic gyroscopes mounted mechanically de-coupled above spinning rings inside a cryostat, measuring deviations from the usual Earth-rotation offset below ~30 K, with rotating low-temperature helium proposed as the physical source of the effect.
brief_reason: What carries across is the decoupled-precision-interferometry scheme that senses a drive-proportional, threshold-onset, sign-asymmetric anomalous signal after ruling out confounds; what is domain-specific is the gyroscope-over-spinning-ring cryogenic-helium apparatus and the Earth-rotation reference.
